item: rs7866411 / rs2093835 (SMARCA2/VLDLR cluster leads) — exact chr:pos not given in main text
description: Main text (p.1348) names these as the two most significant of >5 independent low-frequency SMARCA2/VLDLR resequencing signals with p<1e-3, but gives no chromosome:position. Both rsIDs were searched against S6B (fine-mapping/HaploReg table, 271 rows) and NOT found by rsID — S6B's 'SMARCA2' region label (125 rows) contains a different, expanded candidate SNP set that does not include these two lead SNPs.
impact: chr/pos/coord_build fields for these two rows are null; regulatory annotation (Promoter/Enhancer/DNase) for the specific lead SNPs cannot be attached, only for nearby SNPs in the general genomic region.
resolution_path: Re-open with access to the paper's raw resequencing summary statistics (Mendeley Data DOI 10.17632/98mh8z78m3.1, per Data and Software Availability) or GWAS Catalog cross-reference by rsID.

item: rs34803545 (TYRP1-upstream, phase1 resequencing) — exact chr:pos not given; 'perfectly linked' enhancer/DNase partner SNP not identified by rsID
description: Main text states this SNP is ~600kb upstream of TYRP1 and perfectly linked with a conserved, skin-enhancer/DNase-hypersensitive variant (cited to Table S6), but does not name that partner variant's rsID, and rs34803545 itself was not found in S6B by string search.
impact: chr/pos and the specific regulatory-mark SNP cannot be resolved from the two source PDFs as provided.
resolution_path: Cross-reference dbSNP for rs34803545 coordinates; the partner variant may be resolvable via HaploReg (paper's cited tool) LD lookup around TYRP1 if the exact resequencing summary stats become available.

item: rs2110015 (SNX13) — exact chr:pos not given; absent from S6A and S6B
description: Main text (p.1349) reports this SNP with beta/p-value but no chromosome:position; confirmed absent from both supplementary tables by string search.
impact: chr/pos/coord_build fields null for this flagship non-canonical candidate.
resolution_path: dbSNP lookup for rs2110015 coordinates (straightforward for a future pass with network access to NCBI/Ensembl).

item: EPM2A and FREM1 individual lead SNPs and p-values not named
description: Main text states these two genes are 'above the 95% confidence interval of expected versus observed significance' on the resequencing QQ plot (Figure 5A) but names no specific lead rsID or p-value for either gene individually — only a qualitative visual flag plus the explicit statement that neither has any known pigmentation role in humans or model organisms.
impact: No locus-level rsID/p-value/effect-size row possible for EPM2A or FREM1 individually; both are captured as a single combined row with the exact quoted caveat.
resolution_path: Would require the underlying resequencing GWAS summary statistics (Mendeley DOI 10.17632/98mh8z78m3.1) to identify per-SNP results in the EPM2A (chr6:145,550,394-145,806,673) and FREM1 (chr9:14,724,664-14,910,993) targeted-capture regions (boundaries from Table S7).

item: Two phase-2 tanning-status GWS hits — no rsID/gene/chr:pos given anywhere in source text
description: Main text states two tanning-status associations 'met genome-wide significance' in the phase1+phase2 meta-analysis but explicitly calls them 'most likely spurious' (no LD support, low tanning-status heritability) and names no locus identifiers at all.
impact: Row is retained purely as an honesty flag (genome_wide_significant=True with effector_status=not_a_locus) with zero identifying fields — cannot be treated as a genuine locus for network-building purposes.
resolution_path: Would require the phase1+2 meta-analysis summary statistics; author correspondence may also be needed since the paper itself did not consider these worth naming.

item: Table S5 (Top 50 initial-GWAS associations) — P-value column exponents truncated in PDF extraction
description: Both text extraction and image-crop rendering (dpi=250) of Supplementary PDF pages 5-6 consistently truncate the 'P value' column at the page's right margin, yielding only a mantissa (e.g. '1.08E', '0.000') with no visible exponent for most rows. All other columns (Chr, Position, SNP, Closest_gene, Gene_start, Gene_end, Distance) were fully recovered for 50/50 rows.
impact: Table S5 rows are NOT included as individual locus rows in EXTRACT_Martin2017_loci.csv because the p-value (the whole point of a 'top association' table) cannot be reliably captured. Table S5 also lacks the San/W.AFR/N.EUR frequency breakdown the task schema requires, and it is described in the main text as superseded/incorporated into the phase1+2 novel-variant narrative already captured via S6A/S6B and the main-text prose (this is the source of the SMARCA2 rs10962731/rs146831108, VLDLR rs872257, SLC24A5 rs2470102, TYRP1 rs115075138, and SYNE2/LACTB rows visible in Table S5 but not independently extracted here as they duplicate/are superseded by better-characterized rows elsewhere).
resolution_path: Higher-resolution PDF rendering (e.g. dpi=400+) or direct request of the publisher's supplementary Excel/Word source (if available) would recover the full column; not attempted further in this pass given Table S5's secondary status for this task's schema.

item: S6B 'region' column labels (SMARCA2, TYRP1) are genomically overlapping/non-disjoint
description: The S6B xlsx sheet's own 'region' column groups 125 rows under 'SMARCA2' spanning chr9:2,295,707-14,880,217 (>12Mb, covering three genomically distinct sub-clusters: VLDLR/RP11-125B21.2 at ~2.3-2.6Mb, TYRP1-upstream/RP11-74C3.1/U2 at ~12.05-12.62Mb, and FREM1 at ~14.72-14.88Mb) and 45 rows under 'TYRP1' that substantially overlap the same 12.05-12.62Mb sub-cluster. Table S7 (targeted-resequencing capture regions) gives cleaner, named, non-overlapping boundaries (FLJ35024_reg, TYRP1_reg, FREM1_reg) that were used instead to interpret which S6B rows belong to which biological locus, but the xlsx's own region labels were not re-written/split in the delivered CSV — only referenced in classification_notes.
impact: A downstream user filtering EXTRACT tables by S6B's literal 'region' string alone (rather than by position) would incorrectly merge the VLDLR, TYRP1-upstream, and FREM1 sub-loci.
resolution_path: If S6B rows are pulled directly by a downstream script, re-derive locus assignment from position using Table S7 boundaries (documented in this extraction's spec), not from the S6B 'region' column verbatim.

item: Nama resequencing sample count inconsistency in main text (172 vs 182)
description: The paper's own resequencing paragraph gives two different Nama sample counts in close proximity: one instance states 269 zKhomani and 172 Nama, another nearby states 269 zKhomani and 182 Nama, for the same 441-total-sample resequencing cohort.
impact: Cohort-size metadata for the resequencing analysis carries a +/-10 ambiguity on the Nama side; not used in any numeric extraction field in this CSV (p-values/betas are taken directly from stated results, not recomputed from N), so no downstream numeric field is affected, but documented for provenance completeness.
resolution_path: Cross-check against Table S4 (genotyping/phenotyping matrix), which states Nama Reseq N=170/171 (Pheno/Geno/Reseq rows) — closer to 172 than 182, suggesting 172 may be the more internally-consistent figure, but this is an inference, not a confirmed correction of the source text.
